name: agentdb-learning-plugins
description: Create and train AI learning plugins with AgentDB's 9 reinforcement learning algorithms. Includes Decision Transformer, Q-Learning, SARSA, Actor-Critic, and more. Use when building self-learning agents, implementing RL, or optimizing agent behavior through experience.
capabilities: Create learning plugins using nine reinforcement learning algorithms via AgentDB CLI
Train models with Decision Transformer, Q-Learning, SARSA, Actor-Critic, and five other algorithms
Store and retrieve agent experiences using AgentDB adapter API for training datasets
Implement experience replay and prioritized replay buffers for sample-efficient learning
Optimize training performance with batch processing and WASM-accelerated neural inference
Integrate learning plugins with reasoning agents through AgentDB API
author: DNYoussef
securitySummary: Documentation-only skill containing no executable code. All detected patterns are from instructional code examples showing users how to use the external AgentDB CLI and API. The skill itself performs no network calls, file access, or command execution.
valueStatement: Building self-learning AI agents requires implementing complex reinforcement learning algorithms from scratch. AgentDB Learning Plugins provides ready-to-use access to nine proven algorithms including Decision Transformer and Q-Learning, enabling you to train autonomous agents that improve through experience.
userTitle: Train AI Agents with Reinforcement Learning Algorithms
limitations: Requires Node.js 18 or higher and AgentDB version 1.0.7 or above installed
Basic understanding of reinforcement learning concepts is recommended for effective use
Training performance and speed depend on local computational resources available
Does not include pre-trained models and requires training from experience data

useCases.title: Build Self-Learning Game Agents
useCases.description: Create autonomous agents that improve gameplay performance through experience using Q-Learning or Decision Transformer algorithms.

useCases.title: Experiment with RL Algorithms
useCases.description: Test and compare different reinforcement learning approaches including Actor-Critic and SARSA for academic research purposes.

useCases.title: Optimize Agent Decision-Making
useCases.description: Train agents to optimize decision-making in complex environments with continuous or discrete action spaces for production systems.

promptTemplates.title: Create Basic Q-Learning Agent
promptTemplates.prompt: Use AgentDB Learning Plugins to create a Q-Learning agent plugin for a simple grid navigation task. Show me the configuration parameters and explain how to store training experiences.

promptTemplates.title: Train Offline with Logged Data
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me implement a Decision Transformer plugin using AgentDB to train an agent from historical experience data without requiring environment interaction. Include code for loading logged experiences.

promptTemplates.title: Implement Experience Replay
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to implement experience replay buffers with AgentDB Learning Plugins. Include both standard replay and prioritized experience replay for better sample efficiency and training stability.

promptTemplates.title: Build Multi-Agent System
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a multi-agent training system using AgentDB where multiple agents share learned experiences through a common replay buffer and train a collaborative policy together.

bestPractices: Start with Decision Transformer for offline learning from logged data before attempting online methods that require environment interaction
Use validation splits during training to detect overfitting early and adjust hyperparameters accordingly
Implement experience replay buffers to improve sample efficiency and training stability across all value-based algorithms
antiPatterns: Training without validation data leads to overfitting that goes undetected until the agent is deployed in production environments
Using excessively high learning rates causes training instability, divergence, and failure to converge to optimal policies
Ignoring the exploration-exploitation trade-off by using fixed epsilon values results in suboptimal learned policies

faq.question: Which algorithm should beginners start with?
faq.answer: Decision Transformer is recommended for beginners because it trains stably from logged data without requiring online environment interaction or complex exploration.

faq.question: What are the minimum system requirements?
faq.answer: Requires Node.js version 18 or higher and AgentDB version 1.0.7 or above. Training performance benefits from additional RAM and multiple CPU cores.

faq.question: How do I integrate this with Claude Code?
faq.answer: Import createAgentDBAdapter from agentic-flow/reasoningbank package and initialize with enableLearning set to true for full integration with Claude Code.

faq.question: Is my training data stored securely?
faq.answer: AgentDB stores all data locally in the database path you specify. No training data or experiences are sent to external servers by default.

faq.question: Why is my model training not converging?
faq.answer: Try reducing the learning rate, increasing training epochs, checking that experiences have properly formatted state and reward values, or using a smaller batch size.

faq.question: How does this differ from OpenAI RLHF?
faq.answer: AgentDB focuses on reinforcement learning from environment interactions rather than human feedback. Use Decision Transformer for imitation learning from demonstrations.
